item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Giraffes are very cool creatures and I like them a lot. +100.

Joking aside, I enjoy this as a fun little exercise into the hypothetical. It doesn't overstay it's welcome and it continuously provides new concepts so it doesn't get boring. My favorite bit is most definitely the changing of the phrase on the gate. Does it mean a giraffe apocalypse is coming? Horses are related to giraffes; four giraffemen of the apocalypse perhaps? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I paged through the comments thinking, "Yes, I can be the one to congratulate this SCP for being the logical conclusion to that one Eddie Izzard joke!"

But someone beat me by two years.

Oh well. Have an upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kudos for turning an Eddie Izzard joke into a skip.

I'm wondering what the hell for E. Coli is like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm. I want to see aardvark hell.

"Verrry interesting…"

Is this by any chance near/at Site 2??

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just… wha? +1, no regrets.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So how I understand this is:
There exists a hell where Giraffes are punished. Symbolism? Maybe this is a hell for beings that are embodied as Giraffes? +1, loved it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: True, but I imagine if there were some random door to the any other animal's afterlife (including humans), we would've found some kind of evidence for it. Besides, I'm just pointing out one possible implication of the article. If it doesn't work for you, that's fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absence of evidence is not evidence of absence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thing I love about this is that it actually kinda gave me chills. Sure, it's kinda ridiculous, but the existential implications are a bit creepy. There's a Hell for giraffes. There's redemption for giraffes. We have proof of an afterlife for giraffes, but we don't for humans. Are humans even the dominant species? Do giraffes actually have greater purpose than humans? Look at what you think the purpose is for your life. Now look at a giraffe. That giraffe has much more meaning in the universe than you ever could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So giraffe demons came in, kidnapped the souls in either giraffe hell or giraffe purgatory, and it sounds more like purgatory to me, because one of the things keeping back the giraffe apocalypse failed.

Did I miss anything?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess this one isn't for everyone. That being said, it is definitely for me. I loved it. I hope there is a yellow jacket hell somewhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's hilarious to me; the idea of a bunch of researchers sitting around looking at each other while discussing this makes me laugh.
"So hell exists, but for…"
"Giraffes. Yes."
"…oh…"
I don't know if that's sad or hilarious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is articles like this that make me proud to be a member of this site. You made Giraffe Hell. And it worked. Who said originality is dead?
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite enjoyed this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just love how bizarre this is.
Upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the idea of a giraffe hell, but I especially like the idea that they have a whole prophecy/end-times scenario going on. I upvoted, but I have one nitpick:

[After this point, SCP-1557-B-001 resumed torturing SCP-1557-A instances and refused to answer further questions.]

"Torture" seems a little non-clinical, but maybe that's just me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this so much and feel that now is a good time to mention how giraffes are actually aliens like elephants and are here to observe our species prior to the conquest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alternatively: have you ever seen Steam's "Meet the Pyro" video? It's kinda like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I imagine 173 hell could be replicated by just sending the Eyepods into its containment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So we know about giraffe hell and Futurama showed us Robot hell. I wonder what aardvark hell or 173 hell is like? Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I cannot take this seriously. First off, centiakivas are an abomination that's almost enough to make me downvote on their own. They're silly, borderline offensive, and make no sense. Beyond that, though, it's giraffe hell. And then the Harrowing happened. The sphere's taunts are silly and distracting, and as hells go, it's really a pretty unimaginative one.

Well, to be perfectly fair, it -is- a hell for Giraffes. And they aren't exactly the smartest of animals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, alright. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I give this a tentative up vote, but I agree that it seems just a little crowded.

+1 for giraffe salvation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted at "and b) is not a giraffe".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know, it was just that since they have some camel-like features I thought the tag could apply.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They were lead down the path

They were lead

RAGE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…just, er, more Greenland?

I missed that entirely, and it changed me from neutral to up-vote. I only hope that it was author's intent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was intrigued at the first mention of giraffes, but my expectations really just weren't filled.

item.title: Crazy good.
item.content:encoded: The author had my vote with the mention of giraffes. Deligtfully weird and very imaginative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't even make sense in the context of the Foundation's "universe of anomalies" — it's basically Discworld logic. And also it's inaccurate to the real history of our universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sorry, it's still pulling me out of immersion because it makes no sense to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I initially enjoyed the draft, but no, this is just a garglemesh of clashing styles. You've got too many ideas in a short space. I can't picture it well enough in my head because the descriptions are contradictory where rich and lacking where consistent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty much all of this. :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From Wikipedia:

Camelids are members of the biological family Camelidae, the only living family in the suborder Tylopoda. The extant members of this group are: Dromedaries, Bactrian camels, llamas, alpacas, vicuñas, and guanacos.

Camelids are even-toed ungulates: they are classified in the order Artiodactyla, along with pigs, hippopotamus, deer, giraffes, cattle, goats, antelope, and many others.

Both camelids and giraffes are in the order Artiodactyla, but giraffes are not camelids.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should it also have the ''camelid'' tag (Giraffa camelopardalis)? Since we happen to have a camelid tag it would be sad not to use it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm afraid this is just a little too silly for me at the minute, which is a shame as it's well-written and has a certain surreal humour (indeed, I would upvote as it stands as a -J). Particularly vexing was the description of the location and door - I was unsure why the door to giraffe hell should be burning hot if giraffe hell is cold? I do understand the intent is that human explorers assume it to be a gateway to a Judaeo-Christian human hell, only to have their anthropocentric assumptions violated (incidentally, am I missing something or is the 'hell' through the door just, er, more Greenland?), but not a lot is made of this theme. I will also note that the notion of an object that has a temperature that can only be 'experienced' by making contact with it doesn't make much sense; even if it doesn't radiate heat but only conducts, it would still warm its surroundings by convection as the air contacting it would heat up. I'm also wondering how the door was opened if its contact temperature is equivalent to an arc welder.

The taunts from the 'demons' about the giraffes' long necks being overcompensation also seem strange in a theology where (presumably) giraffes are the primary recipients of divine judgement. It also seems to clash with the semi-portentous tone of the rest of the description. In general I think the article suffers from too many ideas and not enough focus on the implications of each. I could probably be persuaded to upvote with a more in-depth look at the discoverer's assumptions and the punishments the giraffes receive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Action bunnys summon giraffe. The seal has been opened.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still loving it. Fucking giraffes. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love these simple bits of absurdity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like its whimsy
could be tightened up, particularly the punishments.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…what is that supposed to mean? This isn't a joke SCP.
It COULD be made into one, but it seems to be focused on the content rather than the the humor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Crosses the line but doesn't go far enough past it to be funny. Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A hell built for giraffes. Huh.

I agree with everyone on the centiakivas. I don't think it's necessary to include a unit for religious fervor. There are many other ways to acknowledge the need for personnel with certain beliefs (see scp-616).

I think footnote 1 can be incorporated into the main text. You could even rephrase it to stress that the door is breaking a few laws by not radiating heat.

The dialogue could be better. You should make it clear that Fredricks is with the Foundation. Also, the references to the balls of light seem a little inconsistent. You designated one with a unique number, and then referred to another as simply "an instance of SCP-1557-B".

I can't really take this seriously right now, and the centiakivas nearly made me dismiss the entire article. That said, I think this has potential. Change the above mentioned, work on the overall tone a bit, and figure out if you want this to be scary, or just anomalous. I like the idea, but the execution needs work. Withholding vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's almost absurd enough for me to like, but this is too timid. You've just taken Christian theology (although granted the Harrowing of Hell is mildly obscure), and ported it over to giraffes. Remove the silliness, or go far further. This halfway approach is very weak.

Basically this.
It starts out pretty promising, but ultimately seems half-formed, half-finished. I'd say go either dead-serious with it, or farther off the deep end. As it is, it's just too middling to really…be much of anything.

And the centiakivas are pretty awful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I cannot take this seriously. First off, centiakivas are an abomination that's almost enough to make me downvote on their own. They're silly, borderline offensive, and make no sense. Beyond that, though, it's giraffe hell. And then the Harrowing happened. The sphere's taunts are silly and distracting, and as hells go, it's really a pretty unimaginative one.

It's almost absurd enough for me to like, but this is too timid. You've just taken Christian theology (although granted the Harrowing of Hell is mildly obscure), and ported it over to giraffes. Remove the silliness, or go far further. This halfway approach is very weak.

Whatever you do, please drop centiakivas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The -B's didn't really have any reason to care about Foundation operatives, really; I mean, what could they feasibly do? So they just ignored them. Foundation agents more like tried to touch various materials to SCP-1557-B just like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's just a free-standing door.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To quote spikebrennan in this post:

For readers who may not have a background in theological engineering: the akiva (named after http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rabbi_Akiva ), is the ISU unit for fervor. To measure it, one places the subject three meters from a properly calibrated thaumaturgic meter (taking care to perform the measurement in an environment free from thaumaturgically reflective surfaces, such as silver or certain types of minerals such as marble, that may interfere with an accurate reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Centikakivas are dumb in any case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How would you quantify religious fervor?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't forget, you can edit posts instead of replying to them. Simply go to options->edit, and you can change your post however you please!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure that's not the first article to use centiakiva as a fervor unit; SCP-1844 used it first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to admit this is a genius idea, however I'm a little confused. Is SCP-1557-1 just a door in the middle of nowhere? Or is it actually attached to something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A personally designed hell for Giraffes. And then giraffe angels come to rescue them? This is most certainly interesting, but I think I need a few more hours to process this. I'm just going to stay neutral for now, but possibly a +1 for later.
Edit: one of the footnotes says there was testing on what materials passed through SCP-1557-B; so are you saying they willingly got tested? Or did the Foundation throw various materials at them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… what?

Standard units of measurement for religious fervor?! (Which you misspelled the unit at least once, by the way)

A "gateway to hell" with a temp that high that can only be determined by touching it because it doesn't radiate heat but it doesn't melt anything that touches it because it doesn't radiate heat except it does enough that we can measure it except it doesn't. *head explodes*

A gateway that apparently only lets atheists through.

Demons/angels that are really, really stupid at their jobs. I mean, seriously, yelling the equivalent of a "yo mama" joke at a giraffe?

I just… this is far, FAR too much of a baffling mess for me to take seriously. It sounds like something out of a comedy-fantasy book, but one of those cheap ones that's only about 100 pages long because the author couldn't sustain the "plot" any further.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and b) is not a giraffe (Giraffa camelopardalis).

I admit that this piked my interest, because giraffes are wicked cool. But overall I don't get it. I understand it's a hell for giraffes, but I feel it's (or I'm) missing something. So no vote for now.

I don't like the quantitative measurement of religious fervor.

but the primary goal of agents in the area should be to map out the location, if possible.

Why? There's nothing for 1000s of Km!

constructed of an currently

tie poe.

more than 1000km in all directions

''in any direction'' would be better I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: well I read the SCP's "name" in the SCP mainlist and oh my god now I get it jesus christ this is genius

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm confused.
